verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents unanimously identified the United States based on strong visual evidence (English signs, US-style stop sign, mobile homes). The judge correctly followed Path A (consensus) and confirmed the US, despite the ground truth coordinates pointing to a different location (likely a dataset error or mismatch).
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: Perfect consensus across all five agents on the United States. The reasoning was sound, relying on multiple independent lines of evidence (linguistic, regulatory, architectural).
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool consisted solely of the United States, which was the only plausible candidate given the overwhelming evidence. While a diverse pool is usually better, in this case, the evidence was so specific that other countries were implausible.
